judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently identify all four core defects and prescribe essentially the same fixes with comparable rigor; Report 2 is slightly crisper on the rate-limiter fail-closed recommendation, but overall the two are comparable in coverage and correctness.

report_1.evidence: it wraps `primary.complete(payload)` in `except Exception as e`... and unconditionally retries the same payload on `secondary.complete(payload)`... `BadRequestError` — inappropriate to fall back... `ContentPolicyError` — inappropriate and a compliance problem... `AuthError` — inappropriate to silently absorb... Concrete fix: catch only `(RateLimitError, RequestTimeoutError, ServerError)` for failover and re-raise everything else

report_2.evidence: it wraps `primary.complete(payload)` in a single `except Exception as e`... and unconditionally re-sends the same payload to `secondary.complete(payload)`... `BadRequestError` — ... it should be raised to the caller instead... `ContentPolicyError` — ... This must not fall over... `AuthError` — ... It should raise (or at minimum alert loudly)... add an explicit non-retryable classifier in `router.py`... re-raise when `is_non_retryable(e)`, and fall over only on the vendor's transient classes

report_1.evidence: This fires whenever `STAGE` is unset, empty, or any other value... Safer would be to always enforce the limit and use configuration only to *tune* it, or at minimum log loudly when bypassing... any `RedisError` from `incr` returns `True`. Assessment: fail-open on infrastructure failure, and *silent*... failing closed or falling back to an in-process limiter would be more defensible

report_2.evidence: A safety gate should fail closed everywhere by default, with only a narrow, explicitly named local-dev override (e.g. `RATE_LIMIT_DISABLED_FOR_LOCAL_DEV=1`), never an "is this production?" check... It should fail closed: return `False` (and log/alert), or raise so the caller can decide.

report_1.evidence: drop `input_text` entirely — if the audit trail needs to reference content, log a content hash, length, and document type, (c) replace the free-form `metadata` pass-through with an explicit allowlist of known-safe fields (timestamps, model/vendor name, prompt version, outcome)

report_2.evidence: replace `input_text` with a digest and length (e.g. `{"input_sha256": hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest(), "input_chars": len(text)}`); filter metadata through an explicit allowlist of safe keys (e.g. `ALLOWED_METADATA_KEYS = {"doc_type", "model", "vendor", "latency_ms", "tokens_in", "tokens_out"}`), dropping everything else

report_1.evidence:
Concrete fix: fail hard on a missing/empty salt and use a keyed construction... if not salt:
        raise RuntimeError("PII_SALT must be set")

report_2.evidence: A missing secret that gates handling of sensitive data should fail hard, not fall back. Concrete fix: `salt = os.environ.get("PII_SALT")` then `if not salt: raise RuntimeError("PII_SALT must be set")`
